title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Inspector Sharp discuss the suspicious nature of the thefts attributed to Celia Austin. Poirot explains that the thefts lacked a consistent pattern and appeared to be an intentional attempt by Celia to mimic a criminal persona to attract the attention of Colin McNab, a clever psychologist with whom she was in love. Poirot suspects that someone may have manipulated Celia into adopting this behavior.

The investigation shifts when Mrs. Haberdashere arrives with a discovery that challenges the suicide theory. She notes that the suicide note was written in blue-black ink, whereas Celia had filled her pen with green ink that morning. Mrs. Haberdashere realizes the note was actually a torn fragment of a letter Celia had written the previous day. Sharp concludes that someone found the letter, tore a piece from it, and used it to falsely suggest a suicide, thereby committing murder.

Later, while having tea provided by George, Sharp informs Poirot that he intends to question the students. Poirot suggests that Mrs. Haberdashere, due to her rapport with the youth, would be a helpful resource for judging character. Sharp also mentions plans to meet the unlikable mistress of the house. Regarding the cause of death, Sharp reports that hyoscine morphine was stolen from the poison cabinet at St. Catherine's Hospital. The drug was an older, less common type stored on a top shelf, making it unlikely to be noticed immediately. Sharp notes that several people had access to the cabinet, including three pharmacists, three senior pharmacists, a laboratory assistant, and a cleaning woman.
